figures.caption: Fig. 2: The Input uses multi-view RGB-D frames alongside 2D supervision generated by VLM. During Training, the Gaussian Feature Field is learned, where the semantic feature (fsem) is processed by a Semantic Decoder, and the instance feature (fins) is refined via Query-guided Cross Attention. For Inference, our highly optimized rendering pipeline is employed. The key to real-time performance is the Top-K Hard Selection strategy, which leverages the geometric sparsity inherent in Gaussian Splatting to significantly reduce the computational cost of high-dimensional feature accumulation within the SnugBox and AccuTile rasterization process, enabling real-time multi-modal rendering.
Fig. 4: Real-time Sensor Simulation with Fast-SegSim.

Fig. 1: Our real-time rendering speed enables robots to acquire realistic sensor observations and segmentation GT with low latency. The 3D-Consistent GT labels are critical for fine-tuning the Perception Module, effectively bridging the simulation and real-world gaps in embodied AI.
